title: How are souls transferred in Eva
display_title: How are souls transferred in Eva
wikitext:
[]: How are souls transferred in Eva?             Quote Post by RamblinEvaFan » Sat Aug 01, 2009 1:29 pm I know this is probably a question answered many times before, but I'm wondering, when Rei1 dies and Rei2 dies and Rei3 dies, how are the "souls" transferred from each host? Is that hitned at ever in any source-material (offical)? Also what is the concept of the 'soul' in Evangelion? Is it just the same as in the Abrhamaic religions? (I guess that's not saying much since each one of them have divergent ideas of the 'soul').

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sat Aug 01, 2009 2:02 pm You know whats odd I was just wondering about that myself earlier today. *cue Reichu's entrance complete with a reference to Ghostbusters*

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Sat Aug 01, 2009 2:26 pm i know you will hate me but... Quantum magix (lol i dunno) One thing ,if you want some realistic thought on soul-transfer, i think you can assume that soul is something materialistic (in quantum way of thoughts) that you can take,but where it is located is fairly unknown,and when is dissolves after the body died .(i remember a biiiig ATF-discussion thread where this had been a part of discussion,if someone could link/find it ,that would be nice). After they took it they probably used s process kind of similar to soul into eva-technique.Sorta i think.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 01, 2009 4:29 pm More stuff here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3443

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 02, 2009 2:20 pm In the Eva universe, the soul, the consciousness of a person, is something that can be scientifically observed through a field of science known as Metaphysical Biology. They use magic quantum science to move souls around, as they try to do so when Shinji is stuck inside Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by shadowdancer » Mon Aug 10, 2009 12:40 pm AuraTwilight wrote: In the Eva universe, the soul, the consciousness of a person, is something that can be scientifically observed through a field of science known as Metaphysical Biology. They use magic quantum science to move souls around, as they try to do so when Shinji is stuck inside Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 10, 2009 1:22 pm i like the computer-aided neurology idea. only, if that's the case, does that mean humans, through computers, can go from a data description of a soul to "creating" a copy of a soul from that data and "burning it" onto a body's DNA like one burns a CD. which, if you think about it, means humans are basically capable of creating souls. which seems... very god-like to me. well, no wonder lorenz keel thought humanity was too far gone and if i may bring a topic from the rebuild forum to this one (without bringing in any actual spoilers, of course) ... when it comes to souls i'm more curious about how they're "split." because i can easily see that Rei's walking around with lilith's soul while lilith's empty body is down in terminal dogma. likewise i can see that lilith's soul can be transferred from Rei I to Rei II to Rei III as they each die. i'm now wondering, though, what about unit 00? if it's really the soul of "one of the previous Rei clones" within 00 (which would make it Rei I?), how did they manage that? they moved Rei I's soul into unit 00 when she died, but then how did they also put it in Rei II? did they split it? did they just make another "copy" of it? (and couldn't gendo just have "copied" yui's soul and put it in one of the Rei clones and have his wicked way with her? gosh. no, he had to have instrumentality. i guess yui was like the one stubborn soul that just refused to be copied. talked about a scratched disk).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:31 pm They already tell us that they can't copy souls. That's why there's only ever 1 Rei around. As for Eva-00's soul, there's probably more speculation about it in the Eva-00 soul thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:36 pm Rape. Its how you say "Hello, I've got your soul" in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:37 pm Agree... Though I think it is more to Advanced-Computer-Aided-Neurology. Each Rei's body is a container for Rei's 'SOUL' which is stored in NERV's database. Once one Rie died, the database will be used to code new Rei's DNA and neural-pattern, to build a Rei again. But neither the transfer nor build is perfect, so one Rei have different behavior compared to another, though the basics are still the same.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Aug 10, 2009 9:20 pm AuraTwilight wrote: That's another thing. Creating souls is impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Aug 11, 2009 2:33 pm i just had a thought: what about kaworu? he's got adam's soul, right... does that mean the embryo in gendo's hand has no soul within? (would make sense, i guess). if it does have a soul, that means adam's soul was split, right? and if it hasn't got a soul... then is adam's soul not necessary for third impact? (or was the "adam's soul" part of gendo's version of instrumentality supposed to be eva 01's acquired core? but gendo wasn't going to use unit 01 to begin with...? shit, i keep getting confused over my previous confusion).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 12, 2009 5:38 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: Rape. Its how you say "Hello, I've got your soul" in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Aug 12, 2009 6:42 am carla wrote: and of course, the other half of lilith's soul blew up with unit 00-- so is our poor, sad little FAR condemned to never be whole again

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Aug 12, 2009 12:11 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Kaworu has Adam's entire soul. Since he was part of the Giant Naked Rei God-thing during Third Impact, it's implied that Gendo had Kaworu's soul put back in the Adam in his hand.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 12, 2009 12:30 pm i don't know why, but i always thought that was just... lilith-rei giving Shinji what he wanted to see. or, more appropriately, putting things in a way Shinji could understand. kaworu was arguably the one who taught Shinji the biggest lesson regarding angelic nature, so it makes sense to me that Rei would bring kaworu's image at a point where Shinji needs to make sense of things. does that sound like it makes sense at all?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Aug 12, 2009 12:55 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Rei III has the full soul of Lilith. In the Director's Cut of episode 23, Rei and Unit 00 briefly turn into an Eva-sized Giant Naked Rei, which is probably symbolism implied that the soul refused back together.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Wed Aug 12, 2009 1:09 pm Carla wrote: i don't know why, but i always thought that was just... lilith-rei giving Shinji what he wanted to see. or, more appropriately, putting things in a way Shinji could understand. kaworu was arguably the one who taught Shinji the biggest lesson regarding angelic nature, so it makes sense to me that Rei would bring kaworu's image at a point where Shinji needs to make sense of things.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Aug 12, 2009 9:16 pm I'd like to think that Armisael only acted as a catalyst. If the two actually merged, even partially, then Rei's character development is pretty much botched.
